channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: ZRP dismisses viral video linking police assault to returning immigrants at Beitbridge @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: THE Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has dismissed a viral social media video that which naysayers allege are officers assaulting returning immigrants at the Beitbridge Border Post. Police say the assertion is misleading and the video footage is old and unrelated to recent events, whether real or perceived. In a statement, ZRP national spokesperson Commissioner Paul [...] The post ZRP dismisses viral video linking police assault to returning immigrants at Beitbridge appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Nearly 59,000 Zimbabweans Return Home as Government Expands Repatriation Efforts
item.description: Nearly 59,000 Zimbabweans Return Home as Government Expands Repatriation Efforts The Government, working alongside humanitarian organisations, private sector partners and local authorities, has intensified efforts to assist Zimbabweans returning from South Africa, where Afrophobic attacks have forced thousands to flee. Authorities say additional buses, food supplies, temporary accommodation and medical services have been mobilised to [...] The post Nearly 59,000 Zimbabweans Return Home as Government Expands Repatriation Efforts appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Traitors Not Vindicated by Second-Round Vote on CAB3 @263Chat @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: The Zimbabwe Youth Action Platform (ZYAP) has demanded the ruling party Zanu PF to take immediate action against legislators who absconded from the initial vote on Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3), insisting that their later participation does not vindicate what it describes as a [...] The post Traitors Not Vindicated by Second-Round Vote on CAB3 appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Mnangagwa dismisses social media critics @bulawayo24 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: President Emmerson Mnangagwa has dismissed political discourse on social media as detached from electoral realities, telling Zanu PF’s top leadership that while critics remain active online, the rulin... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Returnee surge overwhelms Beitbridge @bulawayo24
item.description: Thousands of migrants are streaming back into Zimbabwe through the Beitbridge Border Post following South Africa’s intensified enforcement of immigration laws, with many returnees arriving exhausted,... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: High Court reinstates dismissed police officer @bulawayo24

item.description: PPC Zimbabwe has put its 418-hectare Arlington Property back on the market after a proposed US$30 million sale to Transvaal Africa collapsed when the buyer failed to pay the purchase price by the agre... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.description: The Bulawayo City Council has recommended approval of a water connection for the Zimbabwe National Road Administration (Zinara)’s Esigodini tollgate, despite an existing council resolution suspending... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwe’s recovery tests resilience @bulawayo24
item.description: Zimbabwe is experiencing one of its strongest periods of macroeconomic stability in years, with inflation falling sharply and the central bank beginning to ease monetary policy. However, economists ca... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwe leads push for Africa travel reforms at UN Tourism summit
item.description: Nqobile Bhebhe, ZIMBABWE has emerged as a leading advocate for travel facilitation and regional tourism integration after successfully championing a landmark resolution that won unanimous support from African member... (via Ebusiness Weekly) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Ebusiness Weekly

item.title: Thousands of Zimbabweans repatriated after occupying Durban’s Shallcross sports field
item.description: By Zainul Dawood l IOL THOUSANDS of Zimbabwean nationals were bused back home after occupying the Shallcross sports field in Durban, South Africa, for four days. On Thursday, surrounded by a strong police presence, buses were seen loading people and their possessions. An official from Zimbabwe, who did not want to be named, said he [...] The post Thousands of Zimbabweans repatriated after occupying Durban’s Shallcross sports field appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: ‘Please don’t come to Russia’: Zimbabwean prisoner of war reveals the human trafficking pipeline
item.description: By United24 Media WHEN Tatenda Tarwire—a 37-year-old Zimbabwean national—received a message from a Moscow-based agent just a few months ago, he thought his proverbial ship had come in. According to him, upon agreeing to uproot himself from Zimbabwe to Russia, he immediately started dreaming of what he would do with the small fortune he would [...] The post ‘Please don’t come to Russia’: Zimbabwean prisoner of war reveals the human trafficking pipeline appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: All set for Total Football International Showcase
item.description: Everything is in place for the highly anticipated Total Football International Showcase, which kicks off today at Churchill High School, bringing together some of Zimbabwe’s most promising footballers and international scouts. The showcase gets underway with assessments for junior players aged between 13 and 17, which will run June 4-5. The senior category, comprising players [...] The post All set for Total Football International Showcase appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
